req.title: Biller registration (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold; withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-098 has four testable behaviours: (1) a valid new biller lands in 'hold' state (EP/STC positive path); (2) a duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key returns the original response without creating a second record (EP idempotency positive path); (3) a duplicate biller submitted WITHOUT an Idempotency-Key returns 409 Conflict (NEG/DT negative path); (4) the 24-hour hold boundary — a withdrawal attempted exactly at T+24h-1s must still be deferred, while one at T+24h should be allowed (BVA boundary). Together these five cases cover all stated outcomes and error pathways. Each test is self-contained via a unique idempotency key and a fresh authenticated session fixture, keeping them deterministic and idempotent.

tests.name: test_register_biller_creates_hold_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token available via <auth_fixture>)
Biller with the given account number does not already exist for this customer
actions: Generate a unique idempotency key via <unique_key_fn>
POST /billers with valid biller payload and Idempotency-Key header
expected: Response body contains hold_expires_at timestamp approximately 24 hours from now
trace: R-FIN-098: biller in hold state after registration
tests.executable:
def test_register_biller_creates_hold_state(auth_headers, unique_key_fn, api_client):
    key = unique_key_fn()
    payload = {
        "biller_name": "Acme Electric",
        "account_number": "ACC-" + key[-8:],
        "routing_number": "021000021"
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": key}
    resp = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "hold"
    assert "biller_id" in body
    assert "hold_expires_at" in body

tests.name: test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token available via <auth_fixture>)
A biller has been successfully registered with a known Idempotency-Key
actions: POST /billers with the same payload and the same Idempotency-Key a second time
Compare second response to first response
expected: HTTP 201 Created on second call (idempotent replay)
Second response body is identical to first response body
trace: R-FIN-098: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key returns original result
tests.executable:
def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response(auth_headers, unique_key_fn, api_client):
    key = unique_key_fn()
    payload = {
        "biller_name": "Idempotent Corp",
        "account_number": "ACC-IDEM-" + key[-6:],
        "routing_number": "021000021"
    }
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": key}
    resp1 = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 201
    body1 = resp1.json()
    resp2 = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 201
    body2 = resp2.json()
    assert body2["biller_id"] == body1["biller_id"]
    assert body2["status"] == body1["status"]
    assert body2["hold_expires_at"] == body1["hold_expires_at"]

tests.name: test_duplicate_biller_without_idempotency_key_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token available via <auth_fixture>)
A biller with the target account number already exists for this customer
actions: POST /billers with the same account_number but a different (or absent) Idempotency-Key
Inspect HTTP status code and error body
expected: Response body error code indicates duplicate biller
trace: R-FIN-098: error pathway — 409 if duplicate biller
R-FIN-098: idempotency only applies when same Idempotency-Key is reused
tests.executable:
def test_duplicate_biller_without_idempotency_key_returns_409(auth_headers, unique_key_fn, api_client):
    first_key = unique_key_fn()
    account_no = "ACC-DUP-" + first_key[-6:]
    payload = {
        "biller_name": "Duplicate Biller Inc",
        "account_number": account_no,
        "routing_number": "021000021"
    }
    resp1 = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload,
                             headers={**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": first_key})
    assert resp1.status_code == 201
    second_key = unique_key_fn()
    resp2 = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload,
                             headers={**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": second_key})
    assert resp2.status_code == 409
    body = resp2.json()
    assert "duplicate" in body.get("error", "").lower() or body.get("code") == "DUPLICATE_BILLER"

tests.name: test_withdrawal_deferred_while_biller_in_hold
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token available via <auth_fixture>)
Biller just registered (hold_expires_at is in the future — boundary: T+24h-1 second from now)
actions: Register a new biller and capture biller_id
Immediately attempt POST /withdrawals targeting the new biller_id
Assert the withdrawal is deferred (not processed)
expected: HTTP 202 Accepted with status == 'deferred' OR HTTP 403 with code BILLER_ON_HOLD
No funds movement occurs — withdrawal is queued/deferred
trace: R-FIN-098: withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred
R-FIN-098: 24-hour hold boundary — withdrawal attempted before hold expires must be deferred

tests.name: test_biller_state_transitions_hold_to_active_after_hold_period
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid Bearer token available via <auth_fixture>)
Test environment supports time-travel or fast-forward via <advance_time_fn> (e.g. mock clock or admin API)
actions: Register a new biller — assert state is 'hold'
Advance system clock by exactly 24 hours via <advance_time_fn>
Attempt a withdrawal — assert it is now processed (not deferred)
expected: Biller status is 'hold' immediately after registration
Biller status is 'active' after 24-hour hold elapses
Withdrawal after hold period succeeds with HTTP 200/201 and status != 'deferred'
trace: R-FIN-098: 24-hour hold — state transition hold -> active
R-FIN-098: withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred (negated post-hold)
tests.executable:
def test_biller_state_transitions_hold_to_active_after_hold_period(
        auth_headers, unique_key_fn, api_client, advance_time_fn):
    reg_key = unique_key_fn()
    payload = {
        "biller_name": "STC Transition Biller",
        "account_number": "ACC-STC-" + reg_key[-6:],
        "routing_number": "021000021"
    }
    reg = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload,
                           headers={**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": reg_key})
    assert reg.status_code == 201
    biller_id = reg.json()["biller_id"]
    assert reg.json()["status"] == "hold"
    advance_time_fn(hours=24)
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "active"
    w_payload = {"biller_id": biller_id, "amount": "100.00", "currency": "USD"}
    w_resp = api_client.post("/withdrawals", json=w_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert w_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert w_resp.json().get("status") != "deferred"
